treatment_information.specific_treatment: TIPS revision and embolization of gastric varix
treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Embolized one of gastric varix with coils and sclerosing agents; mechanical thrombectomy using balloon and cleaner device; angioplasty of TIPS shunt with a 10 mm balloon.
treatment_information.treatment_response: Successful thrombectomy and angioplasty with decreased portosystemic pressure gradient.

treatment_information.specific_treatment: Octreotide, IV pantoprazole, ceftriaxone, lorazepam, Zofran, normal saline
treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Octreotide gtt at 50 mcg/hr; IV pantoprazole, ceftriaxone 1g IV daily; normal saline 1L; lorazepam, Zofran as needed.
treatment_information.treatment_response: Initial management for variceal bleeding; no specific complications mentioned.

treatment_timeline.event: Patient presented with hematemesis and was in stable condition, later showing signs of variceal bleeding.
Patient started on Octreotide drip and received IV medications to manage bleeding.
Patient underwent intubation due to massive hematemesis.
TIPS revision and mechanical thrombectomy procedures were performed.
Follow-up planned for repeat laboratory tests and medication management.

patient_outcome.final_status: Stable at discharge, but requires assistance.
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Follow up at listed appointments and repeat labs.
patient_outcome.complications: Presence of blood clots in leg, spleen, and liver; transient intubation for respiratory management.
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Prognosis considered good; continued monitoring and outpatient therapy recommended.
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Ambulatory with assistance; mental status clear and coherent.
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Patient was informed about the reasons for admission and provided discharge instructions. Expressed appreciation for the care received.
